text_blocks.text: Armenia established a
Church that still exists independently of
both the Catholic and the Eastern
Orthodox churches, having become so in
451 AD as a result of its stance
regarding the Council of Chalcedon.
Today this church is known as the
Armenian Apostolic Church, which is a
part of the Oriental Orthodox
communion, not to be confused with the
Eastern Orthodox communion. During its
later political eclipses, Armenia depended
on the church to preserve and protect
its unique identity. The original location of
the Armenian Catholicosate is Echmiadzin.
However, the continuous upheavals, which
characterized the political scenes of
Armenia, made the political power move to
safer places. The Church center moved
as well to different locations together
with the political authority. Therefore, it
eventually moved to Cilicia as the Holy
See of Cilicia.
